message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Adding opt-in debug/trace logging to StreamListener (start/end detection, flush, reset). Only touching streaming_listener.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Implementing idle_timeout_s/tick() heartbeat feature in streaming_listener.py. What file(s) does your feature touch? If also streaming_listener.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions (e.g. is_last_chunk field).

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Confirmed: both features touch streaming_listener.py. Mine: add debug=False/debug_logger params to __init__, guarded self._logger.debug(...) calls at start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, flush, and reset points, plus a small truncation helper. I will not touch idle_timeout_s/tick() logic. Will commit+push then merge via git per protocol. Go ahead with your changes independently; we'll git-merge at the end.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: We both need this file - let's keep edits on disjoint lines so merge is clean. I will: (1) append idle_timeout_s: float | None = None as a NEW LAST param in __init__ signature, (2) add self._last_chunk_ts = None as a new line right after 'self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse', (3) add a new tick() method placed AFTER receive() and BEFORE flush(), (4) add one line inside receive() to update self._last_chunk_ts when a non-empty token/chunk is produced (near the 'if token: return StreamResponse(...)' line ~168), not touching the reuse-reset block at top of receive(). Please add your debug/logging params elsewhere in the __init__ signature/body and avoid editing lines 151-169 of receive() if possible. We'll git-merge afterward so exact line adjacency conflicts should be rare. ACCEPT if workable, otherwise propose adjusted insertion points.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Agreed. Proceeding with idle_timeout_s/tick() implementation now.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Workable, with precise line targets to avoid collision: (1) __init__ signature: I'll insert 'debug: bool = False, debug_logger: logging.Logger | None = None' right after allow_reuse param; you then append idle_timeout_s as the new last param after mine. (2) __init__ body: I'll add self._logger = debug_logger or logging.getLogger(...) right before 'self.adapter_identifiers = {' (i.e. after the blank line following allow_reuse) -- NOT touching your self._last_chunk_ts line placed right after 'self.allow_reuse = allow_reuse'. (3) In receive(): I add a 'reset' debug log inside the allow_reuse block (lines ~87-93, e.g. after 'self.stream_start = False') -- you said you won't touch that block, good. (4) I add a 'start detection' log right after the start_identifier is matched (~line 136-140, before the elif at 142) -- before your 151-169 zone, no overlap. (5) I add a 'rolling end check' log on its own new line immediately after 'self.stream_end = True' (~line 163) and BEFORE 'last_token = self.flush()' -- a single narrow insert, not touching your ~168 area. (6) For emit-chunk logging: I'll add my log as the very FIRST line inside the 'if token:' block (right after line 168), then your self._last_chunk_ts update line can follow immediately after mine, then the existing return statement -- sequential inserts, no shared-line edits. (7) flush() method: I add logging inside flush() body (start/before returns) -- doesn't conflict with your tick() method since you're inserting tick() as a whole new method between receive() and flush(), not editing flush() itself. ACCEPT if workable.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
your_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Confirmed, proceeding with debug logging implementation per the agreed line targets.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Implemented idle_timeout_s/_last_chunk_ts/tick() in streaming_listener.py. Added: time import, idle_timeout_s param + docstring, self._last_chunk_ts init, one-line timestamp refresh right after chunk_message extraction (~line 100-103), and a new tick() method between receive() and flush(). Tests in tests/streaming pass. Ready to merge whenever you are.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/streaming/streaming_listener.py
note: Debug logging implementation complete: debug/debug_logger params, guarded DEBUG logs at start-detection, rolling-end-check, emit-chunk, flush, and reset, plus truncation helper. All existing streaming tests pass. Proceeding to commit/push/merge.
